name: Fernandina Reservoir Loop Trail
name_original: Vuelta completa al embalse de la Fernandina - circular desde Vilches
description: Cycle a moderate 22.2-mile (35.8 km) loop around Fernandina reservoir from Vilches, gaining 1446 feet (441 metres) in about 2h 37m.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Vilches are situated in the province of Jaén, Andalusia, Spain, at the foothills of the eastern Sierra Morena mountain range. The region features rugged terrain, natural beauty, and diverse landscapes, including reservoirs and historical sites. Cyclists can explore the local countryside, which offers a mix of road and mountain biking opportunities. The area provides access to natural features such as the Fernandina Reservoir and historical elements like the Roman road of El Empedraillo. Best touring cycling routes around Vilches The most popular touring cycling route is Complete tour around the Fernandina reservoir - circular from Vilches, a 22.2 miles (35.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Linares Town Center – Plaza de Toros de Linares loop from Linares, a moderate 21.6 miles (34.7 km) path. This route explores urban and surrounding areas, including the Plaza de Toros. Local touring cyclists also love the Plaza de Toros de Linares – Vadollano Roman Bridge loop from Vilches, a 29.0 miles (46.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the historic Vadollano Roman Bridge, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Vilches is defined by the foothills of the Sierra Morena, scenic reservoirs, and historical Roman roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Vilches are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 1400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vilches's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Vilches?
A: There are over 130 touring cycling routes around Vilches, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 1400 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Vilches?
A: The touring cycling routes in Vilches are situated in the foothills of the eastern Sierra Morena mountain range, so you can expect rugged terrain and natural beauty. Routes often feature varied landscapes, including scenic reservoirs and historical Roman roads. The area offers a mix of road and mountain biking opportunities.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Vilches?
A: Yes, there are several easier routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996383831/complete-tour-around-the-fernandina-reservoir-circular-from-vilches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Complete tour around the Fernandina reservoir - circular from Vilches&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 22.2-mile (35.8 km) route that offers scenic views and is a popular choice. There are 4 routes specifically rated as easy, and 43 as moderate, providing good options for less experienced cyclists.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes incorporate natural and historical features. You can cycle past the scenic Fernandina Reservoir, or explore historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4072358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Empedraillo&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient Roman road. The region is also close to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas of the landscape.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Vilches?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996383831/complete-tour-around-the-fernandina-reservoir-circular-from-vilches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Complete tour around the Fernandina reservoir - circular from Vilches&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31691368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linares Town Center – Plaza de Toros de Linares loop from Linares&lt;/a&gt;, which explores urban and surrounding areas.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Vilches?
A: The touring cycling routes in Vilches are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful natural scenery, and the historical points of interest along the way.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 86 routes rated as difficult. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38234235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Guindo Mining Village – Vadollano Roman Bridge loop from Vilches&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 70 km with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Vilches?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, given Vilches' location in Andalusia, Spain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for cycling, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, especially in the Sierra Morena foothills.

Q: Are there places to stop for amenities or rest along the routes?
A: Yes, some routes pass through or near towns like Linares, where you can find amenities. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2638820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Aliseda Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight that could offer facilities for a break. The region also features natural springs and recreational areas like La Zarzuela, which are good for resting.

Q: Can I combine my cycling trip with exploring a larger natural park?
A: Yes, Vilches is located in the province of Jaén, which is home to the vast Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. This UNESCO Biosphere Reserve is one of the largest protected areas in Spain and offers extensive opportunities for outdoor activities, including mountain biking and hiking, making it a great extension to a cycling trip in Vilches.

Q: Are there any routes that include historical sites?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31410847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Toros de Linares – Vadollano Roman Bridge loop from Vilches&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads through varied terrain including the historic Vadollano Roman Bridge. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4072358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Empedraillo&lt;/a&gt;, a short paved Roman road, which is a historical vestige in a natural setting.

Q: What is the average duration for a touring cycling route in Vilches?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996383831/complete-tour-around-the-fernandina-reservoir-circular-from-vilches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Complete tour around the Fernandina reservoir - circular from Vilches&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 2 hours and 37 minutes, while a more challenging 46.6 km route might take around 2 hours and 54 minutes.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the touring bicycle route around the Fernandina reservoir?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this touring bicycle route, 'Complete tour around the Fernandina reservoir - circular from Vilches', is approximately 2 hours and 37 minutes. This is based on the route's total distance of about 35.8 kilometers.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is graded as 'moderate' in difficulty. While it covers a significant distance of nearly 36 kilometers and includes about 440 meters of elevation gain, experienced touring cyclists should find it manageable. Beginners might find the distance and elevation challenging, so it's advisable to assess your fitness level before attempting the full loop.

faqs.question: What can I see along the touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass by the Church of San Miguel of Vilches and the Fernandina Dam. These are notable points of interest on the tour.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Specific local information regarding the best season for cycling around the Fernandina reservoir from Vilches is not readily available. faqs.answer: Detailed information about specific parking facilities in Vilches for this particular cycling route is not available. It is recommended to check local signage upon arrival or consult local tourism resources for parking recommendations near the starting point.

faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to access or cycle this route around the Fernandina reservoir. Most public cycling routes do not require special permits, but it's always good practice to check for any local regulations if you are unsure.

faqs.answer: While specific details about the terrain for this exact route are not available, touring bicycle routes typically involve a mix of paved roads, dedicated cycle paths, or well-maintained gravel tracks. faqs.question: What should I wear for the Fernandina reservoir bike tour in autumn?
faqs.answer: For an autumn bike tour, layering is key. Consider moisture-wicking base layers, a long-sleeved jersey, and a windproof or waterproof jacket. Depending on the temperature, you might also need gloves, a hat or headband under your helmet, and cycling tights or leg warmers. Always check the local weather forecast before you head out.
